discoveryFaqs: What is Mitingu?
Mitingu keeps planning, registration, communications, attendee management, reporting and Event Intelligence in one place for corporate event teams and agencies.
Who is Mitingu built for?
Mitingu is built for corporate event teams, agencies, marketing teams, communications teams and professional conference organisers running important events.
Is Mitingu only an event registration tool?
No. Registration matters, but Mitingu also covers communications, collaboration, reporting, analytics, white-label delivery and Event Intelligence.
What is Event Intelligence?
Event Intelligence helps teams ask better questions of event data, create useful reports and learn from what happened.
Does Mitingu use artificial intelligence?
Yes. Mitingu includes useful AI features such as Reports Assistant where supported. The point is better answers from event context.
What is Mitingu MCP?
Mitingu MCP lets compatible AI assistants work with approved Mitingu event information and available tools. It is available now.
Can Mitingu work with Claude, ChatGPT or Microsoft Copilot?
Mitingu connects with compatible MCP clients, including Claude and ChatGPT. It is compatible with Microsoft Copilot environments that support MCP, subject to configuration.
What information can an AI assistant access?
That depends on your permissions and enabled Mitingu capabilities. Mitingu MCP is built around approved event context, not unrestricted access.
Does AI make decisions or send communications automatically?
No. People stay in control. The assistant can help answer questions, explain context and prepare outputs where supported.
What Event Intelligence capabilities are available now?
Available capabilities include Reports Assistant, event data questions, report generation, event analytics, NPS reporting and communications analytics where supported.
Does Mitingu support white-label event delivery?
Yes. Mitingu can support branded event environments, sites, emails and admin experiences.
How does Mitingu help with reporting and analytics?
Teams can report on registrations, attendance, feedback, NPS, communications and wider event performance.
